title: Buttondown vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Buttondown vs Constant Contact: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
h1: Buttondown vs Constant Contact: Side-by-Side Comparison (2025)
intro: Choosing between Buttondown and Constant Contact comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Buttondown is built for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown, while Constant Contact targets nonprofits and local businesses. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: Developers And Technical Writers Who Prefer Markdown

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on developers and technical writers who prefer markdown
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on nonprofits and local businesses

faq.q: Is Buttondown better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. Buttondown wins for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown, while Constant Contact is stronger for nonprofits and local businesses. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Buttondown starts at a lower price point ($9/mo vs $12/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Constant Contact to Buttondown is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
